more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Middle East crisis affects 56pc Zim firms: CZI
more.description: THE Confederation of Zimbabwe Industries (CZI) has said 56,1 percent of local firms have reported supply chain disruptions, including delays in the delivery of raw materials due to the impact of the United States of America (USA)-Israel conflict with Iran.Agri-business consulting The USA-Israeli conflict against Iran has created significant disruptions worldwide, highlighting the deep interconnection [...] The post Middle East crisis affects 56pc Zim firms: CZI appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Elon Musk’s Starlink blocked from operating in Namibia
more.description: Namibia has rejected an application by Elon Musk-owned Starlink for a licence to provide satellite internet services in the country, dealing the company a second setback in southern Africa. The Communications Regulatory Authority of Namibia (Cran) announced the decision without giving reasons for its refusal, but noted that Starlink’s Namibian subsidiary did not have local [...] The post Elon Musk’s Starlink blocked from operating in Namibia appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Zimbabwe Central Bank holds interest rate at 35 percent
more.description: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe has left its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 35 percent as it assess the inflationary impact of a global oil price shock while reaffirming confidence that annual inflation will remain in single digits throughout the year. The Monetary Policy Committee, which met today (Tuesday), resolved to stay the course on [...] The post Zimbabwe Central Bank holds interest rate at 35 percent appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Innscor shares seen undervalued as analysts flag hidden asset value
more.description: HARARE – Shares in Innscor Africa Limited could rise by nearly a third over the next year, according to new research that argues the Zimbabwean conglomerate is trading below its intrinsic value due to mispriced assets and temporary earnings pressure. SMRI Institute has initiated coverage of the Victoria Falls Stock Exchange-listed group with a “buy” [...] The post Innscor shares seen undervalued as analysts flag hidden asset value appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .
